name: Stage 2 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 2
Route: Mayan San Remo
Date: March 23-29

Keep the prize wheels and bike wheels spinning as we move it over to Mayan San Remo for Stage 2.

name: TTR Cat Claw Crawl
description: Welcome to the "Monday Morning Cat Claw Crawl" Ride. This ride is based on 2.8 w/kg, with the pace increasing as needed. It may include light workouts. We go faster and more comfortably with the power of the bunch. For "Be stronger"

eventSubgroups.name: TTR Cat Claw Crawl (C)

name: Ascenders Team Asia Morning Group Run
description:
Come and join the Ascenders Team as we put on our running shoes and run
daily with our friends too by starting our day in a healthy way. Research
shows that just 30 minutes of running can have huge benefits on your
short-term and long-term health. So we designed our running events to
motivate athletes to run 30 minutes each day to boost your metabolism and
use those calories. Running will also help you look good and feel stronger.

Our group run is designed with runners of all levels in mind and set the
pace range to be determined by our Zwifter Runners. So find a group pace
that matches how you feel each day and make those steps count for you.
Runners who went beyond the event duration will find that we have selected
courses that will help you earn the Route Badges when you run the full
distance.

So lace up those running shoes as everybody is welcome to join with our
friendly and motivating runners.

# Join Ascenders Team on Facebook:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift
# Ascenders Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh

eventSubgroups.name: Ascenders Team Asia Morning Group Run

name: zFondo Series
description:
Welcome to the ZFondo Series! A fondo is a giant group ride and a go-at-your-own-pace event. This year’s ZFondo series explores every Fondo route on Zwift, bringing together Zwifters looking to take on an epic challenge and push themselves toward new endurance goals.  Each month you’ll have the opportunity to unlock a legacy Zwift Fondo kit. 

RIDE SCHEDULE

ZFondo weekends will take place the last weekend of each month, starting September 26, 2025 and ending on March 30, 2026: 

December 26, 2025 - December 29, 2025
Route: France Classic Fondo
Length: 145.9km (90.7 miles)
Elevation: 1099m (3606’)

name: Stage 6a: Zwift Games: Epiloch
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

name: EZR Weekend Wind Down
description:
All riders are welcome to join the EZ Riders (EZR) for this relaxed pace social group ride. Whether you are new to Zwift or group rides, looking for a recovery/rehab ride, or just looking to ride with a supportive group of friends, this ride is for you. The ride leader will be staying within the advertised average pace of this ride as we explore Zwift’s various worlds. Please respect the ride leader’s pace and ride with the yellow beacon.

Join us on Discord during the ride: https://discord.gg/kmfS4YN 

Facebook Page: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZER.Cycling

#zer #ezr #ezriders

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Seaside Sprint
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

eventSubgroups.name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro (A)
NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro (B)

name: GTR - Alpe Du Zwift Race
description:
Race the full Road to Sky route and finish with a climb up Zwift's most famous road, the Alpe Du Zwift. 

Race in pace categories A-D. 

This race is hosted by the Good Time Riders, GTR, an online bunch of friends from across the world who enjoy riding, racing hard, and generally having a good time together. Everyone is very welcome, you can find us on the Companion App, Facebook and Discord.

name: Zwift Epic Race - Volcano Corcuit CCW
description:
Join us as we take on some slightly more challenging race routes!

For more Epic Races, please join the club on the Companion App: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/a1ea0324-7f4a-46a7-8547-a7a3246e349b/home

All racers will start together, so make sure you're ready at the start!

WHAT IS ZWIFTPOWER? 
For further information on connecting to ZwiftPower, check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note: For your results to show up in ZwiftPower, you must use a heart rate monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter. 

Check out zwift.com/racing for more information on how you can participate in other events offered by Zwift and the community to have a great racing experience.

#zwiftofficial

name: Workout 4: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 4: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This session builds explosive power and increases anaerobic capacity. You’ll tackle short, high-intensity efforts above threshold, each followed by controlled recovery. The goal is simple: hit every interval with focus and intent. This is race-style power—sharp, repeatable, and decisive.

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Mountain Mash
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: ZLDR AYOP Event
description:
Start your day on the right foot or the left. Come to our 5k at your own pace (AYOP). Run, walk, chat, laugh and get your day moving on a positive note! Attitude is 90% of any situation so let’s make this day the best day and get you in the best mindset so you can be the best you!

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Strava Club: https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020
Instagram page: https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/
Discord Invitation (not required for the run)
https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn
ZLDR website for Challenge Months: https://www.zldr.club
Online shop: https://www.sweatydino.com

name: OTR Tempo Group Ride
description:
Come join OTR for our weekly Tempo Group Ride. Courses will rotate weekly on shorter circuit routes so slower riders off the back have a chance to rejoin the group. The Yellow Beacon ride leaders will guide you through a mix of sprints, tempo, and recovery laps. Be careful, the fence is live and will zap you if you fly off the front! The final lap is an optional race pace lap and a chance to give it all you got!

B group will focus on riding together at an average pace between 2.8 - 3.2 w/kg.
D group will focus on riding together at an average pace between 1.8 - 2.2 w/kg.

Join us for some conversation during the ride on our OTR discord channel at: https://discord.gg/WjY7EuAMv4

If you are interested in racing with OTR please reach out to any OTR team member. We are happy to help you meet your racing goals!

name: Stage 2 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 2
Route: Mayan San Remo
Date: March 23-29

Keep the prize wheels and bike wheels spinning as we move it over to Mayan San Remo for Stage 2.

name: Mountain Massif Group Run
description:
Join Mountain Massif for some relaxed group runs. The goal is to simply complete the route and collect the badge.

More information:
For more information check out the Mountain Massif website www.mountainmassif.com, follow on Instagram, Mountain_Massif and join our Zwift Club, by clicking the link below
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b639575d-930f-4de6-a79d-bea3313350bc/join

name: Bikealicious Altitude with Attitude
description:
We are a friendly gathering of cycling enthusiasts. We ride for 60 minutes and throw some badge hunt here and there. Bikealicious assigns new routes every Sunday (EST)/Monday (UTC) to keep the workout fresh and fun for all the shifters. We ride with a rubber band to motivate all skill sets to stay together through challenging routes, resulting in an interactive and dynamic workout. Our group includes riders of all power levels. We welcome all riders. 

Ride On!

name: Workout 4: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 4: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This session builds explosive power and increases anaerobic capacity. You’ll tackle short, high-intensity efforts above threshold, each followed by controlled recovery. The goal is simple: hit every interval with focus and intent. This is race-style power—sharp, repeatable, and decisive.

name: Stage 6a: Zwift Games: Epiloch
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Mountain Mash
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Seaside Sprint
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: zFondo Series
description:
Welcome to the ZFondo Series! A fondo is a giant group ride and a go-at-your-own-pace event. This year’s ZFondo series explores every Fondo route on Zwift, bringing together Zwifters looking to take on an epic challenge and push themselves toward new endurance goals.  Each month you’ll have the opportunity to unlock a legacy Zwift Fondo kit. 

RIDE SCHEDULE

ZFondo weekends will take place the last weekend of each month, starting September 26, 2025 and ending on March 30, 2026: 

December 26, 2025 - December 29, 2025
Route: France Classic Fondo
Length: 145.9km (90.7 miles)
Elevation: 1099m (3606’)

name: Cycle Nation Monday Endurance Masterblaster
description:
Welcome to the Cycle Nation Monday Endurance Masterblaster. Join us for an metric century endurance ride to start your week the right way.
The ride is structured in three parts:
The first tempo section is a steady, controlled effort at 2.8-3.0w/kg to settle in.
Then comes the twist - approximately 10 minutes at 3.0-3.5w/kg.
We finish with a steady block at 2.8-3.0w/kg, with optional sprints, focusing on control, consistency and finishing together.
All riders are welcome to join the event and enjoy the friendly atmosphere. Please follow the ride leaders instructions and group around the beacon. Most importantly, this isn’t a race and we ask that you respect the pace set by the ride leader which will allow for an enjoyable group ride experience for all.
Connect with Cycle Nation:
Visit our website: www.cyclenation.co.za
Join our Facebook Group: https://bit.ly/38TTtAh
Join our Zwift Team: https://bit.ly/3D2Q4zL
Join our Strava Club: https://bit.ly/3pEDkFM
Instagram: https://bit.ly/3pEsdN6

name: Stage 6a: Zwift Games: Epiloch | Advanced
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 4

name: Stage 2 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 2
Route: Mayan San Remo
Date: March 23-29

Keep the prize wheels and bike wheels spinning as we move it over to Mayan San Remo for Stage 2.

name: Team Italy Morning Early Ride
description:
WELCOME TO ALL RIDERS!
Team Italy Morning Early Ride is an easy group ride SUB 2.0 w/kg and NOT a race.
Follow the leader (yellow beacon) instructions during the ride.
The sweeper (red beacon) will support the leader and give help to the riders in the rear of the group.
Your jersey will be autoselected on Team Italy kit and unlock it to the end of the ride.
Please, add tag [ITA] after your name.
This is the Facebook group of Team Italy https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftitaly and Strava club of Team Italy https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwift_italy
Join us! Have fun! Ride On!.

#teamitaly

name: Vikings Valhalla Monday Magic
description:
Come and start your week with the Vikings Valhalla Monday Magic.

This is a great ride for new Zwifters, people looking for some fat burning or people looking for a recovery ride.

We will divide the 45 minutes into 3 15 minute blocks. We start out at an easy pace, and increase the pace a little in each block.

All blocks and our average watts will be in the 1.8-2.2 w/kg area.

eventSubgroups.name: Vikings Valhalla Monday Magic (D)
eventSubgroups.description: #doubledraft

name: zFondo Series
description:
Welcome to the ZFondo Series! A fondo is a giant group ride and a go-at-your-own-pace event. This year’s ZFondo series explores every Fondo route on Zwift, bringing together Zwifters looking to take on an epic challenge and push themselves toward new endurance goals.  Each month you’ll have the opportunity to unlock a legacy Zwift Fondo kit. 

RIDE SCHEDULE

ZFondo weekends will take place the last weekend of each month, starting September 26, 2025 and ending on March 30, 2026: 

December 26, 2025 - December 29, 2025
Route: France Classic Fondo
Length: 145.9km (90.7 miles)
Elevation: 1099m (3606’)

name: Cycle Nation Recovery Ride
description:
Welcome to the Cycle Nation Recovery Ride. Join us for an easy morning coffee ride aimed at Zwifters looking for an easy paced morning group ride. All riders are welcome to join the event and enjoy the friendly atmosphere. Please follow the ride leaders instructions and try group around the beacon. Most importantly this isn't a race and we ask that you respect the pace set by the ride leader which will allow for an enjoyable group ride experience for all.

name: GXY BOOST! INTERVAL GROUP RIDE [3.0-4.0 wkg] – CAT B
description:
Welcome to GXY BOOST!
This is a twice weekly interval event which is targeted at riders looking to develop strength and endurance.
The events are open to all riders not just category B , but also those moving from rides of lower intensity.
BOOST! rides will be generally focusing between Vo2 and Threshold so expect the average wkg to finish between the
advertised power range based on a 75kg rider .
The sessions will be between 60 -90 minutes duration depending on intensity, course chosen or ride leader.
There will be no red sweeping beacon, so if you drop from the group simply continue the workout at your own pace.
We will of course continue to support you throughout the ride, just hang on in there and try and make it through to the
end!
Feel free to join in the fun during the ride by using the messaging system or if you prefer our dedicated BOOST!
Discord channel.
GXY are a friendly and supportive club who strive to help others succeed and we are of course looking forward to
riding with you.
If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally
via our Facebook link.
https://www.facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/
https://www.strava.com/clubs/989894
https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub

eventSubgroups.name: GXY BOOST! INTERVAL GROUP RIDE [3.0-4.0 wkg] – CAT B (B)

name: EVO CC Vortex Interval Ride [~3-3.8w/kg avg]
description:
Welcome to the Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] interval session. These rides are geared towards Zwifters that are looking for a varied content one hour session that can give them a full 5 zone workout. Draft in the group and stay zone 1-3, or move to the front and join the sprints for zones 4 and 5 for short bursts. Average for this ride will be mid to high Category B but it may vary based upon course/leader. There will be keepers at the back, and they will try hard to save dropped riders. The group is based upon high social interaction so bring your best banter. Use the mobile app or a keyboard to communicate in-ride.

#evo

name: EVO CC Flux Ride [1.8 - 2.0w/kg avg]
description:
This is a Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] Social Event and ride for all. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that all riders respect the pace set by the leader (Yellow Beacon). We may include intervals over and below the ride average advertised power range, but we will allow enough recovery and time to regroup after every effort. This is not a race, so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong we would welcome any help offered by stronger riders to bring any dropped riders back to the yellow beacon. We welcome good chat and a social atmosphere for new and experienced Zwifters alike, everyone is welcome.

#evo

name: SZR Early Birdies
description:
Welcome to SZR Early birdies

This is a group ride with the focus on remaining together. You should aim to sit between the yellow and red beacon. Follow ride leader instructions at all times.

We will ride at an average pace of 2.5 w/kg prior to any race finish. The race finish is at the discretion of the ride leader and will vary in length dependent on the ride.

If there are any sprints allowed during the ride please regroup on the yellow beacon immediately after.

After hills we will focus on getting the back of the group together with the front.

#SZR

name: 3R SPARK Steady State Ride [~2.0-2.2 w/kg avg]
description: This is a 3R Group Ride Event (not a race). 3R Spark runs five days a week and is designed to be an accessible and sociable training session, aimed primarily at riders in Cat-D. Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 2.0-2.5 W/kg. The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides. Mondays and Tuesdays are our entry-level steady-state rides, where we aim to hold a steady 2.0-2.2 throughout. Thursdays and Fridays are our interval sessions, designed to help Cat D riders push themselves a little further. For interval sessions we aim to average between 2.2-2.5 for the session, but there will be times above and below. Thursday and Friday sessions often include an optional mini-race at the end. Wednesday is the Taylor Zwift Fan Club ride, a led by the hosts of the popular podcast of the same name – expect a sociable steady-state session with lots to keep you entertained! The TZFC ride is livestreamed at twitch.tv/TaylorZwiftFC. Rides are typically around an hour long. Spark is a great introduction to group rides on Zwift, we are a friendly and supportive group who love to see one another succeed. We always have a sweeper (red beacon) whose wheel you can grab if you get dropped. There’s always plenty of good chat, via in-ride messages or on Discord. Come and join us! Join the 3R Facebook group for more info: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #3R #RideRaceRaise

name: 3R Octane Interval Ride [~3.2-3.6 w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. 3R Octane runs five days a week and is designed to provide a solid and fun training platform for riders.

Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 3.2-3.8 W/kg.

The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides, including weekly recovery, or transitional, rides which are usually on a Monday. Short, high intensity intervals, longer sweetspot intervals, flying laps, long and short climbs and exploring little used routes all feature.

The rides are typically around an hour long, but some can be up to two hours long, and all end with a mini race.

The Group welcomes rider interaction through messaging and the Octane discord server and features a super bunch of regulars to which newcomers, and riders stepping up from lower intensity rides, are always welcome. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/

#Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise #3r

name: 3R VOLT Interval Ride [~2.6-3.2 w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. 3R VOLT runs five days a week and is designed to provide a solid and fun training platform for riders.

Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 2.8-3.2 w/kg.

The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides and training needs, the following is a typical week:
Monday – After what has hopefully been a fun backed weekend on the bike, this our recovery session
Tuesday – Tends to be more of a classic interval session, riders usually finish with an average of around 3.0w/kg
Wednesday – We take to the hills and one of our tougher sessions with riders averaging around 3.2w/kg
Thursday – is our no red beacon ride and again tough interval sets and riders finishing around 3.2w/kg
Friday - is a little different, we don't take ourselves too seriously and often play a variety of games and quizzes along side the intervals and riders end up with around 2.8-3.0w/kg

The rides are typically around an hour long, but some may take up to 75mins with the majority finishing with either a ramp or a mini race.

The group welcomes rider interaction through messaging and the VOLT discord server and features a friendly bunch of regulars to which newcomers, and riders stepping up from lower intensity rides, are always welcome. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/

This group ride is not a race so. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.

This group ride is not a race so. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Workout 4: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 4: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This session builds explosive power and increases anaerobic capacity. You’ll tackle short, high-intensity efforts above threshold, each followed by controlled recovery. The goal is simple: hit every interval with focus and intent. This is race-style power—sharp, repeatable, and decisive.

name: GXY SURGE [1.6-2.5wkg] – CAT D
description:
Welcome to GXY Surge!
 
Different ride leaders will target varying effort levels each day, yet still bring you home well within the advertised power!
 
The ride is looking to push Cat Ds and low Cat Cs that little further all within a manageable 50 minute ride.  Please respect the leader’s pace and watch your fitness develop!
 
GXY are a friendly and supportive club who strive to help others succeed.  We are looking forward to riding with you.
 
If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally via our Facebook link or by the email below:
 
https://www.facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/
https://www.strava.com/clubs/989894
https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub

name: Stage 6a: Zwift Games: Epiloch
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 2
Route: Mayan San Remo
Date: March 23-29

Keep the prize wheels and bike wheels spinning as we move it over to Mayan San Remo for Stage 2.

name: SZR Sunrise Ride
description:
Welcome to SZR Sunrise

This is a group ride with the focus on remaining together.
The average pace PRIOR to any race finish will be 2.2 wkg. Suitable for D riders looking to become C’s and above
Sprints and intervals will be added at the discretion of the leader.
We will at times ride above and below the advertised pace, please don’t feel the need to tell the leader they will be aware!

Please regroup quickly after sprints, intervals or hills and sit between the yellow and red beacons.
This is not a recovery ride, we work hard and work as a team.

eventSubgroups.description:
The focus is on the Group and keeping together while having fun to make the time fly fast!
The ride tempo is shown next to the ride category, this is rider weight / gender dependant.
Note that the average is for the ride prior to any optional faster finish (with a beacon).
The ride leader will aim (prior to any faster finish) to ride mid parameters after a short warmup period.
There might be slight variations above or below the average during the ride.

name: Monday Roll Out
description:
The QueenBees are a group of women who share a love of cycling. Just as a hive works together to bring out the best in every member of the community, so do we. We strive to support, empower and encourage women to live their best lives, no matter where they are on their life, cycling or fitness journey. Join us for a social ride, all abilities welcome pace will be around 1.5-1.8 wkg. During the ride try to stay with the yellow lead beacon and follow instructions from your ride leader.

Join our QueenBee hive here (ladies only): https://www.facebook.com/groups/218570110355444

name: Bikealicious Rubberband Joy Ride
description:
This is a social ride welcoming all riders. Our mission is to push you further than before, build your endurance, and have fun. If you are feeling strong, push to the front and drag the group, if not, just sit in the peloton.

We have the “rubber-band” activated, which means that it’s a no drop ride - no matter what level you are, you will be able to keep up, and the group WILL STAY TOGETHER. Note: The overall speed may be lower than in other rides due to the banding effect.

So hop on that bike, grab those bottles, and let’s ride!

name: zFondo Series
description:
Welcome to the ZFondo Series! A fondo is a giant group ride and a go-at-your-own-pace event. This year’s ZFondo series explores every Fondo route on Zwift, bringing together Zwifters looking to take on an epic challenge and push themselves toward new endurance goals.  Each month you’ll have the opportunity to unlock a legacy Zwift Fondo kit. 

RIDE SCHEDULE

ZFondo weekends will take place the last weekend of each month, starting September 26, 2025 and ending on March 30, 2026: 

March 27, 2026 - Mar 29, 2026
Route: Gran Fondo 2022
Length: 92.5km (57.7 miles)
Elevation: 1116m (3661’)

name: Kiwi Crew Riot Race
description:
This race is a Handicap Chaser / Chop style race using Zwift Power Pacer Categories. D Cat start first followed by C\-B\-A with customized time gaps in between. The goal is to work as a Team to reach the finish first to claim line honours, can D stay away or will the other categories chase them down before the finish?

Now one of the established major weekly races on the Zwift Calendar every Monday night so get your week off in the best way! the Riot Race sees a regular Crew battling for line honours every week. Auto Cat Join is on and make sure you have a Zwift Power account \(www.zwiftpower.com\) to be included in the official results!

\* Everyone with a Power Meter and / or Smart Trainer is welcome to join.
\* New cadence rules in play.
\* Winners must have their Strava account linked and are subject to verification checks / requests.

Make sure you check out our other 2 rides, the Kiwi Crew Ride Thursday's at 6:30pm NZ, and the Wakey\-Wakey ride, Monday's at 6:00am NZ . Come and join in the fun!

 https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftnz

\#kiwicrew

name: ZZRC Bring a Bidon Social Ride
description:
Welcome to our ZZRC Bring a Bidon 2.4-2.7. This is a group ride, not a race, where we encourage riding together.

Our rides are open to all riders, the category enforcement merely allows us to ensure a safe and quality ride for you the riders and for our group.

In 2015 ZZRC was founded and are one of the oldest established clubs on Zwift .

YELLOW BEACON will pace between 2.4-2.7 on the ride , with a possible increase of watts on inclines or sprints , but the average for the whole ride will be as advertised

We strongly promote group riding so on most of our rides we will use the Fence to help encourage that. Using a fence ensures that the ride is not over paced .

In the event of falling off the back of the main group , you will need to pace well over group pace to bridge the gap and catch .

This is where our awesome ZZRC sweeps and Ride Buddies will help you return to group using their draft. They will give you clear encouraging instructions to aid this process .

Our crew are a caring bunch who dedicate a lot of hours to this club , but want you to have the best ride experience on Zwift

You will be fully supported and encouraged throughout the ride by the Leader, Sweeps and Ride Buddies .

Our rides do not allow the use of TT Bikes as they do not draft and will require you to push harder on the ride, and in these instances our Sweeps will not be able to help you .

Mountain bikes operate very similarly, minimum draft so more watts (effort) will be required for the ride and any chase backs to group .

All our rides have a 30 minute late join feature which allows you to rejoin if you have technical issues or you find yourself out of the group in the early stages of the ride. Using this feature SHOULD teleport you back into the main group with our YELLOW BEACON .


We love to have a chat on our rides so join us
on Discord: https://discord.gg/QdeS3PtXTX


TOP TIPS

1) Stay close to the ride leader (or just in front on climbs), don’t let a gap form and the elastic snap!

2) Stay away from the Fence – We don’t like to fry anyone

3) Most important of all . . . HAVE FUN AND ENJOY THE RIDE.

#zzrc

Don’t forget to join our ZZRC-We Pace Not Race Facebook page and our club on the companion app

name: WKG PR Recon Ride – Route Preview
description:
This ride follows the provisional route* of the upcoming WKG Progression Riders stage on Sundays.

PR LIVES ON DISCORD — JOIN THE GROUP
All riders, chat, club info, and questions live here:
https://discord.gg/2vYXgYzgvJ
Ride voice channel:
https://discord.gg/ZhVdwkJH8w

C Group – Social Recon (2.8-3.2 w/kg)
• Led group
• Stay together
• Leader announces upcoming PR segments*
• No scoring / no segment contests
• Riders may test shorter segments that doesn’t split the group

B Group – Free Recon
• Open pace – no fixed w/kg and no fence
• Ride your own effort or form groups naturally on the road
• No guaranteed beacon
• If a leader is present, they will announce segments* and can serve as a voluntary regroup point

This is your advantage lap.
Learn the route.
Scout the segments.
Refine your tactics.
Arrive on race day prepared — not surprised.

* The ride also serves as route validation. Length, segments, and final route may be adjusted if needed.

name: GXY MONDAY COFFEE CRUISE [2.3-2.8wkg] – CAT C
description:
Welcome to GXY Monday Coffee Cruise!
Our weekly steady state ride here to ease you into the week ahead.
We will achieve this together by providing a consistent pace to help you build both your strength and stamina at the
lower to middle area of the Cat C range of power.
The ride leader and support team will after 60 minutes bring you to the end of the ride within the advertised power
range! (expect an approximate 2.5 -2.6wkg ride average depending on body size and course selection)
The event is designed to maintain or increase base level fitness of riders. Please respect the leader’s pace and watch
your fitness develop!
GXY are a friendly and supportive club who strive to help our riders achieve their fitness goals.
Our Club emphasis is based on inclusivity, diversity, respect and providing fun at all levels on Zwift whether riding
socially, racing your legs off or hanging around our optional chat groups waiting for our IRL meet ups!
We are of course looking forward to riding with you.
If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally
via our Facebook link:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/
https://www.strava.com/clubs/989894
https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub

name: AERO Fit and Fun Steady State
description:
The AERO Fit and Fun Steady State is a group ride with some optional fun along the way. This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon (Yellow) instructions, if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist riders at the back of the bunch.  

All riders are welcome, we aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your triaining and to improve your skills on Zwift.

Come join us in the green & gold (kit unlocked through completing this ride!)

Spin the legs and have a chat and if you're keen to get involved in racing in our community join us on ZwiftPower or message us via our social media accounts.

https://linktr.ee/aero.racing.team

eventSubgroups.description:
The AERO Fit and Fun Steady State is a group ride with some optional fun along the way. This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon (Yellow) instructions, if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist riders at the back of the bunch.  

All riders are welcome, we aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your triaining and to improve your skills on Zwift.

Come join us in the green & gold (kit unlocked through completing this ride!)

Spin the legs and have a chat and if you're keen to get involved in racing in our community join us on ZwiftPower or message us via our social media accounts.

https://linktr.ee/aero.racing.team

name: ZLDR Easy 30
description:
Join Zwift Long-Distance Runners (ZLDR) run club for a relaxed 30 minute run. Whether you want to use this time to walk just to get your blood flowing or push for a new personal best or anything in between, this is a great session to join! Even if you can’t join for 30 minutes, come for whatever you have available. We are a flexible group of walkers/runners who want to help you get consistent and get results.

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Strava Club: https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020
Instagram page: https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/
Discord Invitation (not required for the run)
https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn

#zldr_run

name: PACK Run: 5k Monday
description:
Join PACK as we start Mondays off with a self-paced, easy run to loosen the legs for the week. The leader will pace with the group, but feel free to run ahead or behind.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftpack/

name: Le ride des Copains bleus
description:
Le mode élastique favorise la cohésion au sein du groupe, permettant à chacun d’exprimer pleinement ses compétences. Ainsi, les défis ne deviennent plus des obstacles, mais plutôt des occasions de partage convivial et de plaisir collectif, transformant l’effort en une expérience enrichissante.

Discutez sur Discord /  Discuss on Discord: https://discord.gg/HgFb6SThT
Notre Facebook: https://m.facebook.com/groups/907351331715445

"Please add “(Gang)” at the end of your username in your Zwift profile.   Merci de mettre (Gang) à la fin de votre pseudo dans votre profil zwift 
"
Ruberband mode encourages group cohesion, allowing everyone to fully express their abilities.      Challenges are no longer obstacles, but opportunities for friendly sharing and collective enjoyment, turning effort into a truly rewarding experience.

name: Workout 4: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 4: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This session builds explosive power and increases anaerobic capacity. You’ll tackle short, high-intensity efforts above threshold, each followed by controlled recovery. The goal is simple: hit every interval with focus and intent. This is race-style power—sharp, repeatable, and decisive.

name: Mountain Massif structured training: Aerobic Stretch 3 (75-92%)
description:
Created by Matt Rowe, from Rowe & King Coaching, this structured training session is designed to help you cycle at a higher intensity, and absorb surges in effort - essential attributes for any Zwift racer. 

For coaching and all your training needs visit https://roweandking.com/


About the session:
Progressive warmup, followed by 3 blocks of ramped efforts - 75-92% of FTP across 12-minutes.  The first two steps ramp up, whilst the final ramp is regressive and ramps down.  4-minutes recovery between blocks.  A warm down completes the workout.


Safety Note:
Only undertake vigorous exercise if you are fit and healthy to do so. If you are at all unsure, consult a Physician or Doctor first.  All training equipment including Bikes and Turbo trainers should be in full working order. Do not use any piece of equipment that does not come up to the required standard.


More information:
For more information check out the Mountain Massif website www.mountainmassif.com, follow on Facebook, Instagram (Mountain_Massif) and join our Zwift Club by clicking the following link https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b639575d-930f-4de6-a79d-bea3313350bc/join.  To record all your training data and chart your progress, consider using the TrainingPeaks app (www.trainingpeaks.com/).

eventSubgroups.name: Mountain Massif structured training: Aerobic Stretch 3 (75-92%) (E)

name: Stage 6b: Zwift Games: Crit Cade | Women Only
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

name: TBR Ride and Shine
description:
Mental Wellness and Sprints!!!
TBR Ride and Shine is a group ride at 1.8-2w/kg, with some Mental Wellness chat and optional sprint efforts and re-grouping.
Each ride there will be wellness topics of interest to hopefully promote a positive mindset with tools to fight a ferocious black dog.
We have a sweep crew so please shout out if you need some help, but this flat route should keep riders nice and tight.
We don't use the fence for this ride as we believe all riders should exercise self-control and respect the ride leader by riding with the yellow beacon at all times. Your co-operation helps us provide everyone with a fun, supportive and inclusive ride.
Follow The Big Ring (TBR) at www.thebigring.cc

eventSubgroups.name: TBR Ride and Shine (D)
eventSubgroups.description:
TBR Knights of Suburbia is a group ride focusing on wellness, consistent with a category C ride at 1.8-2.2w/kg. 
A social roll with some quality mental wellness chat. Each ride there will be topics of interest to hopefully promote a positive mindset with tools to fight a ferocious black dog. 

WE ROLL DEEP. WE ROLL FOR BODY. WE ROLL FOR MIND. WE ROLL FOR OURSELVES. WE ROLL FOR OTHERS. WE ROLL TOGETHER. WE ROLL APART. BUT WHEN WE ROLL, WE KNOW WE’RE NEVER ALONE. Don’t forget, GET YOUR SILLY ON!    

We don't use the fence for this ride as we believe all riders should exercise self-control and respect the ride leader by riding with the yellow beacon at all times. Your co-operation helps us provide everyone with a fun, supportive and inclusive ride.

Follow TBR at thebigring.cc to learn more about our events, read ride reports and leave feedback.

#tbr

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Champs Elysees
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Flanders Endurance Series
description:
Flanders Endurance Series is focused on long distance rides/races.
The global distance of these fondo's will always be around 100km or 2.30h depending on the course of the day.
Use these type of rides to build up your foundation and/or stamina ...
Do you have to finish the ride ? No, that’s not necessary, only when you want to end up in the results you have to do the « whole » ride !
Is this a race ? There will be results displayed for those who like to race it, but you can also tackle it as a great build-me-up endurance training.
Late join is possible.

Heart rate monitors are necessary when you want to end up in the ZP results !

It’s also a great opportunity for you to earn those missing ROUTE badges

#keepmoving #BZReCyclingClub #jerseyunlock

Spotify playlist : BZR`s FTP Booster
Facebook Group : https://m.facebook.com/groups/bzrecyclingclub/

name: Coffee Lovers Let's Start the Week
description: A new week has begun! And we welcome you to a morning or even for someone an evening ride! There is no need to be afraid of Mondays, though they say it has to be a hard day! Our team will cheer you up and fill you with the energy of a strong and aromatic morning coffee! We hope that you will have fun and enjoy this hour with us, not as at a forced training session, but as in the lobby of an old and familiar cafe in the warm company of friends! Our pace will be 2,0-2,2 w/kg, the fence will be active.

eventSubgroups.name: Coffee Lovers Let's Start the Week (D)

name: Fearless Ladder for a Faster 5k
description:
Want to improve your 5K run time. Join us regularly for this ladder workout and watch your results improve.


We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.” – Aristotle<https://everydaypower.com/aristotle-quotes/>


Join for the challenge and group run fun!


Hosted by Team Fearless. To hear about our other rides and runs or learn more about our women's team, head to our Facebook Fans page at https://www.facebook.com/groups/TeamFearlessZR/

#fear

name: Stage 2 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 2
Route: Mayan San Remo
Date: March 23-29

Keep the prize wheels and bike wheels spinning as we move it over to Mayan San Remo for Stage 2.

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Glasgow Crit Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Ride Together
description:
Soyez les bienvenu(e)s dans le ride du club BIKES (tag : BIKES)
But : le mode elastic permet de rester ensemble quelque soit les watts développés. Les plus faibles sont assurés de rester avec le leader !
Ce n’est pas une course.
Rejoins-nous ! ajoute le tag BIKES à ton pseudo Zwift et rejoins le club BIKES FRANCE
sur Companion

Discutez sur Discord: https://discord.gg/a8wYvgwOpens a new window

Notre Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZwiftBikesOpens a new window

Rejoignez-nous : https://www.bikesfrance.com/Opens a new window

Welcome to the BIKES club ride (tag: BIKES)

JOIN OUR NEW CLUB for non-french riders on Companion : club BIKES WORLD,
English spoken !
The idea is to bring together riders from all over the world, no matter where you come from, no matter your level, no matter if you are already in a club or not ; just have fun !

GOAL: Elastic mode allows everyone to stay together regardless of the watts generated. The weakest riders are guaranteed to stay with the leader!
This is not a race.

eventSubgroups.name: Ride Together (E)
eventSubgroups.description:
FRENCH :

Soyez les bienvenu(e)s à la sortie de groupe de la Team BIKES France (tag : BIKES-FR)

« Rouler seul c’est bien mais rouler en groupe et entre amis c’est encore mieux »

Rejoignez-nous tous les Lundi après midi pour un ride groupé à allure libre d'une durée de deux heures, il est ouvert à tous et tous les niveaux.
Le but de cette sortie est de pédaler tous ensemble dans la bonne humeur
On roule en peloton = ce n’est pas une course !

Discutez sur Discord avec nous pendant la sortie sur salon vocal Club Ride :
https://discord.gg/PaSg2bs9Tt

Découvrez-nous sur Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZwiftBikesFrance

Rejoignez officiellement la team BIKES-FR et profiter d’entraînements et de sorties privées et/ou des courses:
https://www.bikes-france.com/index.php/zwift/devenir-bikes-fr

ENGLISH :

Welcome to the BIKES Team France group ride (tag : BIKES-FR)
"Riding alone is good but riding in a group and with friends is even better".

Join us every Monday afternoon for a group ride at a free pace for two hours, it is open to all and all levels.
The goal of this ride is to pedal all together in a good mood
We ride in a pack = it's not a race!

Discuss on Discord with us during the ride on Club Ride voice room:
https://discord.gg/PaSg2bs9Tt

Discover us on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZwiftBikesFrance

Officially join the BIKES-FR team and enjoy private training sessions and rides and/or races:
https://www.bikes-france.com/index.php/zwift/devenir-bikes-fr
Official website :
https://bikes-france.com

name: Monday Run Club
description:
Start your week off right with a group run hosted by the Zwift Runners Facebook Group. We’ll ease into another week of training alongside the community while actively recovering from any long runs you might have had during the weekend. With runs in seven time slots with four pace groups each (A being the fastest and D being the slowest), there’s an event for everyone. 

Pace Groups
A) 7.5–8.5 mph // 12–14 kph
B) 7–7.5 mph // 10.5–12 kph
C) 6–6.5 mph // 9.5–10.5 kph
D) 5–5.5 mph // 8–9 kph

We’ll be using workout mode throughout these runs so everyone can easily determine their speed. Though these runs utilize a mostly steady pace, there will be some speed changes for variety.

name: Ascenders Brunch Bunch Ride
description:
Welcome to [ASC] - Beanie's Brunch Bunch for a chilled ride around the virtual roads of Zwift. Each week the course will vary so, whether you're a beginner just starting out on your first group rides, recovering from injury, or just looking for a steady paced ride then come and join us. We aim to provide a safe encouraging ride for you to enjoy. You’ll be very welcome in the Brunch Bunch

This is a group ride and not a race so please respect the pace and ride with the group. The fence will be used to aid regrouping and politely remind those out front to ease back to the main group before the 60 second countdown ends. If you're a stronger confident rider then ride behind the Lead Beacon and lend a draft for others who may need it.

Ascenders Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwiftOpensOpens a new window a new window

Ascenders Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh

name: Run 10K Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the longest running daily led running event on Zwift. This was the event that kickstarted the NoblePro Running Club in support of a world record attempt at consecutive daily10k runs.
The event has a healthy group of leaders who are friendly and chatty and will help you achieve your goal, not matter your experience. Feel free to join in the chat and become addicted like we all are.

Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it's not a race.
If you don't want to run 10k then come along for as long as you wish, the support is always appreciated.
The event runs at the same time each day, each day of the week there is a different course.

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/

https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: zFondo Series
description:
Welcome to the ZFondo Series! A fondo is a giant group ride and a go-at-your-own-pace event. This year’s ZFondo series explores every Fondo route on Zwift, bringing together Zwifters looking to take on an epic challenge and push themselves toward new endurance goals.  Each month you’ll have the opportunity to unlock a legacy Zwift Fondo kit. 

RIDE SCHEDULE

ZFondo weekends will take place the last weekend of each month, starting September 26, 2025 and ending on March 30, 2026: 

December 26, 2025 - December 29, 2025
Route: France Classic Fondo
Length: 145.9km (90.7 miles)
Elevation: 1099m (3606’)

name: Stage 6b: Zwift Games: Crit Cade
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade
